zh-CN.title: Swift 6.3 experimentalCGen 指南：SwiftPM 原生支持 C 代码生成
zh-CN.description: Swift 6.3 引入了 experimentalCGen 特性，允许 Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM) 的 Build Tool Plugin 生成 C 源码并直接参与 C 模块编译。本文详细讲解其优势及迁移指南。
zh-CN.content:
TL;DR: Swift 6.3 引入了 experimentalCGen 特性，允许 Swift Package Manager 的 Build Tool Plugin 生成 C-family 源码并直接参与 C 模块的编译。这消除了混合语言项目中对外部脚本的依赖，使完整的构建图谱得以在 SwiftPM 内闭环。

Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM) 很早就能管理 Swift、C、C++、Objective-C 等不同语言的源码，也能通过 Build Tool Plugin 在构建过程中运行生成器。但在 Swift 6.3 之前，这两件事之间一直有一个缺口：插件生成出来的 C-family 产物，很难自然地进入 C 模块的编译流程。

Swift 6.3 的 experimentalCGen 正是在补这个缺口。它让 SwiftPM 的 Build Tool Plugin 可以为 C module target 生成 C source files，并支持生成 module maps、header files 和 API notes。对混合语言 package 来说，这不是一个小功能，而是 SwiftPM 能否承载真实原生构建流程的关键一步。

The Challenge: 过去生成代码游离于 SwiftPM 之外

很多原生库的构建过程都不是简单地“把源码编译一下”。在真正进入 C、C++ 或 Objective-C 编译前，它们通常还需要先跑一段生成流程。

常见例子包括：

  • 把 Shader、Kernel 或字节码资源转成 C 数组。
  • 把 Schema、IDL、协议描述文件转成 .c 和 .h。
  • 生成 module map、桥接 header 或 API notes。
  • 把平台相关的静态数据转换成可以被链接器处理的源码。

这些步骤本来就属于构建图的一部分。但过去 SwiftPM 很难完整表达它们。插件可以运行，C-family target 也可以存在，可是插件产出的 C 源码和头文件并不能稳定地被当作该 target 的正式输入。

于是很多项目只能选择绕路：

  • 提前生成代码并提交到仓库。
  • 在 swift build 前额外执行 Shell 脚本。
  • 保留一套 CMake、Xcode project 或自定义打包流程。
  • 把真实构建逻辑藏在 CI 或 release 任务里。

这些做法都能解决眼前问题，但代价很明确：SwiftPM 不再是完整的构建入口，而只是消费某个中间结果。本地、CI、Xcode、发布流程之间也更容易出现行为差异。

The Approach: experimentalCGen 改变了什么

启用这个能力的方式是在 Package.swift 的 tools version 中加入实验标记：

1

// swift-tools-version: 6.3;(experimentalCGen)

开启后，SwiftPM 可以把 Build Tool Plugin 生成的 C-family 文件纳入 C module target 的正常构建流程。也就是说，生成器不再只是一个外部预处理步骤，而是 package build graph 的一部分。

我们可以用数据流来更直观地理解这个变化：


图 1：Swift 6.3 experimentalCGen 构建流程示意图（由 AI 辅助生成）

flowchart TD
    Sources[Package Sources / Resources] -->|Build Tool Plugin| Generated[Generated C-family Files]
    Generated -->|SwiftPM Target Build| CModule[C / C++ / Objective-C Module]
    CModule -->|Link| Product[Swift Package Product]
    
    classDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;
    classDef process fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0288d1,stroke-width:1px;
    
    class Sources,Generated,CModule,Product default;

这个变化看起来很朴素，但它解决的是构建系统里最重要的问题之一：依赖关系能不能被工具准确理解。

当生成输入、生成工具、生成输出和最终 target 都处在 SwiftPM 的描述范围内，构建系统才知道什么时候该重新生成，什么时候该重新编译，哪些文件属于哪个 target，Xcode 和命令行是否在构建同一件东西。

Implementation Deep Dive: 图像处理库的迁移案例

考虑一个图像处理类 Swift package。它对外提供 Swift/Objective-C 可调用的 API，内部依赖 C++ 实现，还需要在构建时把若干计算资源编译成 C 数据，再链接进最终库。

在旧方案里，这个项目通常会拆成几层：

  • 底层原生代码由外部构建系统处理。
  • 生成资源由脚本提前产出。
  • Swift package 只包装已经准备好的结果。
  • 示例 App、CI、发布脚本各自维护一部分构建知识。

这种结构最大的问题不是“复杂”，而是信息分散。真正决定构建结果的规则没有集中在 Package.swift 和 target graph 里。新成员要理解构建链，需要同时看脚本、CI、Xcode 设置和发布文档；任何一个地方漏掉一步，都可能得到不同的产物。

迁移到 Swift 6.3 的 experimentalCGen 后，这类项目可以换一种组织方式：

  • 真实源码继续保留在 package target 中。
  • 需要生成的输入文件作为 package source 或 resource 管理。
  • Build Tool Plugin 负责在构建时调用生成器。
  • 生成出来的 C 源码进入同一个 C-family target。
  • swift build、swift test、Xcode 消费 package 时走同一套构建关系。

这样做不需要公开底层算法、资源内容或内部目录结构。关键点只是：原来散落在外部脚本里的生成步骤，被放回了 SwiftPM 能理解的构建图中。

Results and Metrics: 它解决了哪些实际问题

第一，减少 Generated Source 污染。
生成文件通常不适合人工 Review，也容易造成无意义冲突。把生成过程纳入 SwiftPM 后，仓库可以只保留源输入和生成器，把机器产物留在构建目录。

第二，统一本地、CI 和 Xcode 构建。
如果生成步骤在 SwiftPM 外部，不同环境很容易跑出不同结果。experimentalCGen 让生成步骤成为 target 构建的一部分，命令行和 Xcode 更容易使用同一套规则。

第三，降低混合语言 Package 的发布成本。
很多 Swift package 并不是纯 Swift。它们可能有 C++ core、Objective-C bridge、二进制依赖、资源编译和平台差异。过去这些项目经常需要额外维护打包产物或外部构建系统。现在，至少对一部分生成 C 代码的场景，SwiftPM 可以直接承载更多真实构建逻辑。

第四，让 Plugin 的价值延伸到 C-family target。
SwiftPM Plugin 原本就适合做代码生成和构建前处理。experimentalCGen 让这套机制不再只适用于 Swift 源码或普通资源，而是可以进入 C/C++/Objective-C 模块的核心构建路径。

Lessons Learned: 为什么这是一个里程碑，以及当前的限制

迈向真实工程复杂度

Swift 生态正在面对越来越多混合语言工程：图像处理、音视频、AI 推理、数据库、编译器工具、嵌入式、跨平台 SDK、系统库绑定。这些项目很少是纯 Swift，它们需要构建系统能够理解 C-family 源码、生成代码、二进制依赖和平台规则。

如果 SwiftPM 只能舒服地处理纯 Swift package，它就很难成为这类项目的主入口。experimentalCGen 的意义在于，它让 SwiftPM 朝真实工程复杂度迈进了一步。

这个里程碑主要体现在三点：

  • 构建图更完整：生成 C 产物不再只是外部脚本的副作用。
  • 工具体验更一致：同一套 package 描述可以服务命令行、CI 和 Xcode。
  • package 边界更大：一些过去必须依赖预生成产物或额外打包流程的库，可以重新评估是否直接以 source package 形式分发。

Swift 6.3 同时还提供了 Swift Build 集成预览。SwiftPM release notes 明确提到，experimentalCGen 同时适用于当前 Native Build System 和 SwiftBuild。这一点很重要，因为它说明这不是只为旧构建系统补洞，而是在为 SwiftPM 未来的统一构建模型铺路。

实验边界与限制

experimentalCGen 仍然是实验特性。官方文档已经列出一些限制：

  • 同一个 target 只能有一个生成的 module map。
  • module map 引用的 header 必须和 module map 位于同一目录。
  • 如果生成了 module map，就不能再从 public headers path 提供另一个 module map。

因此，它更适合边界清楚、输入稳定、输出明确的生成流程。比如：

  • 固定资源转 C 源码。
  • Schema 或协议描述转 C/header。
  • 小规模 module map/header 生成。
  • 服务单一 target 的构建期代码生成。

它还不适合被当成万能构建系统替代品。复杂原生工程仍然需要认真设计 target 边界、依赖关系、工具可发现性和 CI 验证。

Next Steps: Package 作者该如何选择

如果你的 Swift package 现在仍然依赖外部脚本生成 C/C++/Objective-C 构建输入，可以用下面几个问题判断是否值得尝试 experimentalCGen：

  • 生成输入是否能放在 package 内部管理？
  • 生成输出是否只服务于明确的 target？
  • 生成器是否能在 SwiftPM Build Tool Plugin 中稳定运行？
  • 生成文件是否不值得提交到仓库？
  • 本地、CI、Xcode 是否都需要同一套生成结果？

如果这些问题的答案大多是肯定的，那么 Swift 6.3 的 experimentalCGen 很可能能简化你的构建链。

它真正带来的不是“少写几行脚本”，而是把真实构建过程放回 SwiftPM 可以理解、调度和验证的位置。对 Swift package 生态来说，结合 Semantic Versioning 语义化版本，这是从轻量依赖管理走向复杂原生工程管理的重要一步。

References

  • Swift 6.3 Released
  • SwiftPM 6.3 Release Notes
  • Swift Build Preview

en.title: Swift 6.3 experimentalCGen guide: SwiftPM natively supports C code generation
en.description: Swift 6.3 introduced the experimentalCGen feature, which allows the Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM) Build Tool Plugin to generate C source code and directly participate in the compilation of C modules. This article explains its advantages in detail and provides a migration guide.
en.content:
TL;DRIntroduced in Swift 6.3experimentalCGenThis feature allows the Swift Package Manager's Build Tool Plugin to generate C-family source code and directly participate in the compilation of C modules. This eliminates the need for external scripts in mixed-language projects, enabling a complete build graph to be closed within SwiftPM.

The Swift Package Manager (SwiftPM) has long been able to manage source code from different languages ​​such as Swift, C, C++, and Objective-C, and can also run generators during the build process through the Build Tool Plugin. However, prior to Swift 6.3, there was always a gap between these two things: the C-family artifacts generated by the plugin were difficult to naturally enter the compilation process of C modules.

Swift 6.3experimentalCGenThis is precisely what it's filling. It allows the SwiftPM Build Tool Plugin to generate C source files for C module targets, and supports generating module maps, header files, and API notes. For hybrid language packages, this is not a small feature, but a crucial step in enabling SwiftPM to support a true native build process.

The Challenge: In the past, generated code was outside of SwiftPM.

The build process for many native libraries is not simply "compiling the source code". Before actually entering the C, C++ or Objective-C compilation, they usually need to run a generation process first.

Common examples include:

  • Convert shader, kernel, or bytecode resources into C arrays.
  • Convert Schema, IDL, and protocol description files into.c and .h。
  • Generate module maps, bridging headers, or API notes.
  • Convert platform-related static data into source code that can be processed by the linker.

These steps are inherently part of the build graph. However, SwiftPM previously struggled to fully represent them. Plugins could run, and C-family targets could exist, but the C source code and header files produced by the plugins could not be consistently used as formal input for that target.

As a result, many projects had to choose to take a detour:

  • Generate code in advance and commit it to the repository.
  • exist swift buildExecute an additional shell script beforehand.
  • Retain a CMake, Xcode project, or custom packaging process.
  • Hide the actual build logic within CI or release tasks.

These approaches can solve the immediate problems, but the cost is clear: SwiftPM is no longer a complete build entry point, but merely a consumer of an intermediate result. Behavioral differences are also more likely to occur between local, CI, Xcode, and release workflows.

The Approach: What Did Experimental CGen Change?

The way to enable this capability is inPackage.swiftAdd the experiment markup to the tools version:

1

// swift-tools-version: 6.3;(experimentalCGen)

When enabled, SwiftPM can incorporate the C-family files generated by the Build Tool Plugin into the normal build process of the C module target. In other words, the generator is no longer just an external preprocessing step, but part of the package build graph.

We can use data streams to understand this change more intuitively:


Figure 1: Schematic diagram of the Swift 6.3 experimentalCGen build process (generated with AI assistance)

flowchart TD
    Sources[Package Sources / Resources] -->|Build Tool Plugin| Generated[Generated C-family Files]
    Generated -->|SwiftPM Target Build| CModule[C / C++ / Objective-C Module]
    CModule -->|Link| Product[Swift Package Product]
    
    classDef default fill:#f9f9f9,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px;
    classDef process fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0288d1,stroke-width:1px;
    
    class Sources,Generated,CModule,Product default;

This change may seem simple, but it addresses one of the most important problems in building systems: whether dependencies can be accurately understood by tools.

When the input, build tools, output, and final target are all within the scope of SwiftPM's description, the build system knows when to rebuild, when to recompile, which files belong to which target, and whether Xcode and the command line are building the same thing.

Implementation Deep Dive: A Case Study of Migrating an Image Processing Library

Consider an image processing class in a Swift package. It provides a Swift/Objective-C callable API, internally relies on C++ implementation, and requires some computational resources to be compiled into C data during the build process before being linked into the final library.

In the old approach, this project was typically broken down into several layers:

  • The underlying native code is handled by an external build system.
  • The generated resources are produced in advance by the script.
  • Swift packages only wrap results that are already prepared.
  • Example App, CI, and release scripts each maintain a portion of the build knowledge.

The biggest problem with this structure isn't its "complexity," but rather the dispersion of information. The rules that truly determine the outcome are not centralized.Package.swiftAnd in the target graph. New members need to understand the build chain by simultaneously reviewing scripts, CI, Xcode settings, and release documentation; missing any step in any of these areas can result in different outcomes.

Migrating to Swift 6.3experimentalCGenLater, these types of projects can be organized in a different way:

  • The actual source code remains in the package target.
  • The generated input files need to be managed as package source or resource.
  • The Build Tool Plugin is responsible for calling the generator during the build process.
  • The generated C source code enters the same C-family target.
  • swift build、swift testXcode uses the same build process when consuming packages.

This approach avoids exposing the underlying algorithms, resource content, or internal directory structure. The key point is simply that the generation steps, which were previously scattered in external scripts, are now integrated into the build graph that SwiftPM can understand.

Results and Metrics: What practical problems did it solve?

First,Reduce contamination from the Generated Source.
Generated files are often unsuitable for manual review and can easily lead to meaningless conflicts. By incorporating the generation process into SwiftPM, the repository can retain only the source inputs and generators, leaving the machine artifacts in the build directory.

second,Unify local, CI, and Xcode builds.
If the generation process occurs outside of SwiftPM, different environments can easily produce different results.experimentalCGenBy making the build process part of the target build, it's easier to use the same set of rules in the command line and Xcode.

third,Reduce the release cost of hybrid language packages.
Many Swift packages are not pure Swift. They may contain C++ cores,Objective-C bridgeBinary dependencies, resource compilation, and platform differences. Previously, these projects often required additional maintenance of packaged artifacts or external build systems. Now, at least for some scenarios that generate C code, SwiftPM can directly handle more of the actual build logic.

fourth,Extend the value of the plugin to the C-family target.
The SwiftPM Plugin is well-suited for code generation and pre-build processing.experimentalCGenThis mechanism is no longer limited to Swift source code or ordinary resources, but can now be accessed through the core build path of C/C++/Objective-C modules.

Lessons Learned: Why this is a milestone, and current limitations

Towards Real-World Engineering Complexity

The Swift ecosystem is facing an increasing number of mixed-language projects: image processing, audio and video, AI inference, databases, compiler tools, embedded systems, cross-platform SDKs, and system library bindings. These projects are rarely pure Swift; they require build systems that can understand C-family source code, generate code, binary dependencies, and platform rules.

If SwiftPM can only comfortably handle pure Swift packages, it will be difficult for it to become the main entry point for such projects.experimentalCGenThe significance lies in the fact that it takes SwiftPM a step closer to real-world engineering complexity.

This milestone is mainly reflected in three aspects:

  • A more complete construction graphGenerating C artifacts is no longer just a side effect of external scripts.
  • A more consistent tool experienceThe same package description can serve command line, CI, and Xcode.
  • The package has a wider scope.Some libraries that previously had to rely on pre-generated artifacts or additional packaging processes can be re-evaluated as to whether they should be distributed directly as source packages.

Swift 6.3 also includes an integrated preview of Swift Build. The SwiftPM release notes explicitly mention this.experimentalCGenIt applies to both the current Native Build System and SwiftBuild. This is important because it shows that this is not just patching up the old build system, but paving the way for a unified build model for SwiftPM in the future.

Experimental Boundaries and Limitations

experimentalCGenThis is still an experimental feature. The official documentation has listed some limitations:

  • The same target can only haveoneThe generated module map.
  • The header referenced by the module map must be located in the same place as the module map.Same directory。
  • If a module map is generated, another module map cannot be provided from the public headers path.

Therefore, it is more suitable for generation processes with clear boundaries, stable inputs, and explicit outputs. For example:

  • Converting fixed resources to C source code.
  • Translate the schema or protocol description to the C/header.
  • Small-scale module map/header generation.
  • Build-time code generation for a single target.

It is not yet suitable as a universal replacement for build systems. Complex native projects still require careful design of target boundaries, dependencies, tool discoverability, and CI verification.

Next Steps: How should package authors choose?

If your Swift package still relies on external scripts to generate C/C++/Objective-C build inputs, you can use the following questions to determine if it's worth trying.experimentalCGen：

  • 生成输入是否能放在 package 内部管理？
  • 生成输出是否只服务于明确的 target？
  • 生成器是否能在 SwiftPM Build Tool Plugin 中稳定运行？
  • 生成文件是否不值得提交到仓库？
  • 本地、CI、Xcode 是否都需要同一套生成结果？

如果这些问题的答案大多是肯定的，那么 Swift 6.3 的 experimentalCGen 很可能能简化你的构建链。

它真正带来的不是“少写几行脚本”，而是把真实构建过程放回 SwiftPM 可以理解、调度和验证的位置。对 Swift package 生态来说，结合 Semantic Versioning 语义化版本，这是从轻量依赖管理走向复杂原生工程管理的重要一步。

References

  • Swift 6.3 Released
  • SwiftPM 6.3 Release Notes
  • Swift Build Preview
